attack_chain.description: Attacker discovered a publicly writable S3 bucket belonging to the LA Times, likely through scanning for misconfigured/leaky S3 buckets.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: an Amazon AWS S3 bucket belonging to the LA Times had been left wide open, granting global write-access to anyone

evidence_chunks.quote: an Amazon AWS S3 bucket belonging to the LA Times had been left wide open, granting global write-access to anyone
an Amazon AWS S3 bucket belonging to the LA Times had been left wide open, granting global write-access to anyone

attack_chain.description: Attacker leveraged the misconfigured S3 bucket with global write access (no credentials needed) to gain access to the hosted JavaScript content.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: granting global write-access to anyone who fancied dropping their code on the server

evidence_chunks.quote: granting global write-access to anyone who fancied dropping their code on the server
granting global write-access to anyone who fancied dropping their code on the server

attack_chain.description: Attacker modified a JavaScript file in the S3 bucket, injecting Coinhive cryptomining code into the Homicide Report site.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: meddled with a Javascript file. In this way, Coinhive cryptomining code was added to an LA Times website

evidence_chunks.quote: meddled with a Javascript file. In this way, Coinhive cryptomining code was added to an LA Times website
meddled with a Javascript file. In this way, Coinhive cryptomining code was added to an LA Times website

attack_chain.description: Malicious Coinhive JavaScript executed in visitors' browsers when they loaded the Homicide Report page.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Coinhive cryptomining code was added to an LA Times website

evidence_chunks.quote: Coinhive cryptomining code was added to an LA Times website
Coinhive cryptomining code was added to an LA Times website

attack_chain.description: The miner was throttled to reduce CPU impact and avoid detection by site visitors.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the miner was throttled to reduce the impact on visitors’ CPUs and make it harder for users to spot that something was awry

evidence_chunks.quote: the miner was throttled to reduce the impact on visitors’ CPUs and make it harder for users to spot that something was awry
the miner was throttled to reduce the impact on visitors’ CPUs and make it harder for users to spot that something was awry

attack_chain.description: Coinhive script hijacked CPU resources of website visitors to mine cryptocurrency (browser-based cryptojacking).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: It is still their computers’ resources that are being hijacked, and their electricity and battery life which is being used

evidence_chunks.quote: It is still their computers’ resources that are being hijacked, and their electricity and battery life which is being used
It is still their computers’ resources that are being hijacked, and their electricity and battery life which is being used

initial_access_vector: Misconfigured S3 bucket with global write access allowed an unauthenticated third party to modify hosted JavaScript files.
persistence_mechanism: Malicious Coinhive JavaScript remained embedded in the LA Times Homicide Report site content served from the misconfigured S3 bucket, persisting since Feb 9 until discovered.
privilege_escalation: None - the S3 bucket already permitted anonymous global write access, so no escalation was required.

techniques.evidence_quote: it has become easier than ever for anyone to identify leaky Amazon AWS S3 buckets
an Amazon AWS S3 bucket belonging to the LA Times had been left wide open
granting global write-access to anyone who fancied dropping their code on the server
meddled with a Javascript file... Coinhive cryptomining code was added
the miner was throttled to reduce the impact on visitors’ CPUs and make it harder for users to spot that something was awry
their computers’ resources that are being hijacked, and their electricity and battery life which is being used
